Me humble name is Rafzan. Good old goblin name meaning honest, generous and nice person, I swear!
Most goblins so afraid of everything, that they fight everything. Me different. Me just want trade.
You humans are so big, strong, clever and beautiful. Me really feel little and green beside you. Must be sooo fun to be human. You surely always make profit!
To be honest to me human friend, me only heard about it, never seen one. I imagine it's something cute and cuddly.
Swamp is horrible. Slowly eating away at health of poor little goblin. No profit here at all. Me will die poor and desperate, probably eaten by giant mosquitoes.
Beardmen are nasty. Always want to kill little goblin. No trade at all. Not good, not good.

So much to do, so little help. Me poor goblin desperately needs help. Me have a few tasks me need to be done. I can offer you all money I made if you only help me a little with stuff which is easy to strong smart human but impossible for poor, little me.

Me heard Thais is big city with king! Must be strong and clever, to become chief of all humans. Me cannot imagine how many people you have to beat up to become king of all humans. Surely he makes lot of profit in his pretty city.

They are mean and cruel. Humble goblin rarely trades with them. They would rather kill poor me if not too greedy for stuff only me can get them. Still, they rob me of it for a few spare coins and there is noooo profit for poor goblin.

Me job merchant is. Me trade with all kinds of things. Me not good trader though, so you get everything incredibly cheap! You might think me mad, but please don't rip off poor goblin too much. Me has four or five wives and dozens of kids to feed!

Humans so clever. Much, much smarter than poor, stupid goblin. They have big rich town. Goblin lives here poor and hungry. Me so impressed by you strong and smart humans. So much to learn from you. Poor goblin only sees pretty city from afar. Poor goblin too afraid to go there.

Me have seen a gold coin once or twice. So bright and shiny it hurt me poor eyes. You surely are incredibly rich human who has even three or four coins at once! Perhaps you want to exchange them for some things me offer? Just don't rob me too much, me little stupid goblin, have no idea what stuff is worth... you look honest, you surely pay fair price like I ask and tell if it's too cheap.

Furry guys are strange fellows. Always collecting things and stuff. Not easy to make them share, oh there is noooo profit for little, poor me to be made. They build underground dens that can stretch quite far. Rumour has it the corym have strange tunnels that connect their different networks all over the world.

I feel uncomfortable and rather unsafe in Thais, so I don't really travel there.
Um... I don't think so.
Although it's the flourishing trade centre of Tibia, I don't like going there. Too much competition for my taste.
It's almost time to journey on.
Kings, queens, emperors and kaliphs... everyone claims to be different and unique, but actually it's the same thing everywhere.
I am a travelling trader. I don't buy everything, though. And not from everyone, for that matter.
I am Rashid, son of the desert.
Of course, old friend. You can also browse only armor, legs, shields, helmets, boots, weapons, enchanted weapons, jewelry or miscellaneous stuff.
Elves... I don't really trust them. All this talk about nature and flowers and treehugging... I'm sure there's some wicked scheme behind all this.
My beloved hometown! Ah, the sweet scent of the desert sands, the perfect shape of the pyramids... stunningly beautiful.
I have to go to Carlin once in a while, since the queen wishes to see my exclusive wares in regular intervals.
Cormaya? Not a good place to make business, it's way too far and small.
It's not the real thing, but almost as good. The merchants there claim ridiculous prices, which is fine for my own business.
Ah yes, Edron! Such a lovely and quiet island! I usually make some nice business there.
Too few customers there, it's not worth the trip.
Um... I don't think so.
I don't like being underground much. I also tend to get lost in these labyrinthine dwarven tunnels, so I rather avoid them.
When you avoid the slums, it's a really pretty city. Almost as pretty as the governor's daughter.
Um... I don't think so.
I like the settlement itself, but I don't set my foot into the jungle. Have you seen the size of these centipedes??
Um... I don't think so.
I wish it was a little bit warmer there, but with a good mug of barbarian mead in your tummy everything gets a lot cosier.
I feel uncomfortable and rather unsafe in Thais, so I don't really travel there.
Um... I don't think so.
Although it's the flourishing trade centre of Tibia, I don't like going there. Too much competition for my taste.
It's almost time to journey on.
Kings, queens, emperors and kaliphs... everyone claims to be different and unique, but actually it's the same thing everywhere.
Your power of observation is stunning. Yes, I'm a rat.
Meep? I mean - hello! Sorry, |PLAYERNAME|... Being a {rat} has kind of grown on me.
Come visit my little pawnshop! General equipment and such. Don't miss it!
Just between you and me hun, that woman is a witch. Love potions, poison, voodoo dolls to torture your neighbour. You name it, she got it.
Uh! <whispers> There are ... rumours, you know? About bloody rituals and gruesome curses ... better don't talk aloud about such issues.
Kshh!! No talks about ... the magic folk here ... This might draw their attention.
Hard to imagine that this fat man has such a pretty daughter.
Don't mention HIM in my house you fool. You call evil and mayhem upon us.
I am not dealing with food.
I won't say anything bad about such a high-born person. That's why I remain silent hun .
I run this little pawnshop, hun, but I think you knew that already.
What interest could a king have in this little settlement? If a king cares about us, there must be something important behind all this.
Liberty comes, liberty goes, only the bay stays. Hehe.
I sell torches, candelabra, and oil.
That guy is a shrewd trader, sweetie. I bet even I could learn a thing or two from him.
I have shovels, picks, scythes, bags, ropes, backpacks, plates, scrolls, watches, some lightsources, and other stuff. Just ask me for a {trade}.
Mostly, pirates are bad for business. But sometimes, they are good for business.
Sadly the workers have hardly enough money to buy something to eat and to drink. That is bad also for my business.
Ah, there is little in the world that comes close to the taste of rum.
I heard there is a fine bounty on his head. Would be a shame though to lose such a handsome man to the executioner <winks>.
Sugar lets the money flow. We depend heavily on it.
Thais might be a splendid town, but this is of no use to us here. The situation in our town gets worse with every ship full of foreigners that arrives here.
I heard Venore will soon acquire several trade monopolies that will grant them wealth for centuries.

Well, <lowers the voice to a whisper> there are several people around who know this or that about voodoo and not all of them are up to something good. Just buy some lucky charms from that old hag Charlotta to be on the safe side.
A handsome man but a bit too stiff. I think no one has ever had a chance to see him smiling.
I will pay you 5 gold for every empty vial. Ok?
Hmm, but please keep Tibia litter free.
You don't have any empty vials.
Don't forget to always have a rope with you! Buy one here, only the best quality!
Don't complain to ME when you fell down a hole without a rope to get you out! You can buy one here now!
Everything an adventurer needs!
A rope is the adventurer's best friend!
Fresh meat! Durable provisions! Ropes and shovels!

Feeling like a bit of treasure-seeking? \z
		Buy a shovel or a pick and investigate likely-looking stone piles and cracks!
Richard. Just Richard. Lost my surname with my past in that storm. <winks>

This lovely island here. Once I didn't have to live off squirrels, it became quite enjoyable. \z
			Nasty things though live underground, so take care where you tread and ALWAYS have a rope with you!
<shrugs> Never been, mate. Heard it's kinda cute, though.

You know, I really don't want to poke into someone else's private life. \z
			Suffice it to say that everyone has chapters of his life he doesn't want to mention. \z
			Judging by Coltrayne's looks, we're looking at a trilogy here.

Old Inigo was the one who found me, actually, and brought me to the outpost. \z
			I was half starved by then. He taught me how to make better traps and how to fish... I owe much to Inigo.

Can you believe how old he is? He won't say it, \z
			but I wouldn't be surprised if he had been around for loooooong time.

Don't talk to ME about squirrels! <shudders> Had to live off them the first days, \z
			when they were the only thing to go into my self-made acorn traps. Nasty.

Don't know what to make of him. Great researcher in all Dawnport matters, though. \z
			Always has a quest or two where he needs help, if you're looking for adventuring work.

Quiet little lady, her. Knows her way around the isle, looking for herbs and stuff \z
			but mostly spends her time in the temple, helping younglings like you choose a vocation.

You know, on the day that ol' pirate decides to make off with all that gold in the bank, \z
			I'm gonna come with him. Should be much more fun landing on a strange island with some gold \z
			to spend on booze and babes! <winks>

Tries to act tough, but he's quite a witty and decent bloke who wouldn't hurt a fly. \z
			We enjoy a good laugh together in the evenings.
This tower, of course, silly one. It holds my {master'}s {treasure}.
His name is none of your business
I am guarding the treasures of the tower. Only those who pass the {test} of the three sigils may pass.
I am known as the riddler. That is all you need to know.
I am the guardian of the paradox tower.
The key of this tower! You will never find it! A malicious plant spirit is guarding it!.
<mumbles>
Just great. Getting stranded on a remote underground isle was not that bad but now I'm becoming a tourist attraction!
Have you noticed that I'm actually the only rock on this island with a proper job? Those lazy pebbleheads! I'm proud to announce: I'm a trader
No, you got it all wrong! I said I'm stuck between a rock and a hard place!
I can help you buy trading stuff with you. Good for me, good for you. It's a win-win!
Have you noticed that I'm actually the only rock on this island with a proper job? Those lazy pebbleheads! I'm proud to announce: I'm a trader
So receive the protection of the twist of fate, pilgrim.
Fine. You are free to decline my offer.
Keep your adventurer's stone well.
Ah, you want to replace your adventurer's stone for free?
Here you are. Take care.
No problem.
Ah, you want to replace your adventurer's stone for 30 gold?
Here you are. Take care.
Sorry, you don't have enough money.
No problem.
You are hurt, my child. I will heal your wounds.
You aren't looking that bad. Sorry, I can't help you. But if you are looking for additional protection you should go on the {pilgrimage} of ashes or get the protection of the {twist of fate} here.

Whenever you receive a lethal wound, your vital force is damaged and there is a chance that you lose some of your equipment. With every single of the five {blessings} you have, this damage and chance of loss will be reduced.

There are five blessings available in five sacred places: the {spiritual} shielding, the spark of the {phoenix}, the {embrace} of Tibia, the fire of the {suns} and the wisdom of {solitude}. Additionally, you can receive the {twist of fate} here.
I see you received the spiritual shielding in the whiteflower temple south of Thais.
I can sense that the druids north of Carlin have provided you with the Embrace of Tibia.
I can see you received the blessing of the two suns in the suntower near Ab'Dendriel.
I can sense that the spark of the phoenix already was given to you by the dwarven priests of earth and fire in Kazordoon.
I can sense you already talked to the hermit Eremo on the isle of Cormaya and received this blessing.
You can ask for the blessing of spiritual shielding in the whiteflower temple south of Thais.
The druids north of Carlin will provide you with the embrace of Tibia.
You can ask for the blessing of the two suns in the suntower near Ab'Dendriel.
The spark of the phoenix is given by the dwarven priests of earth and fire in Kazordoon.
Talk to the hermit Eremo on the isle of Cormaya about this blessing.
Don't forget to deposit your money here in the Global Bank before you head out for adventure.
Check out my good and sturdy weapons!
I am Romella and I will be serving you today.
I have many weapons to offer. If you'd like to see my offers, ask me for a {trade}.
I sell and buy weapons. If you'd like to see my offers, ask me for a {trade}.
Our warehouse is the main supplier of the local garrison.
It says the amazons are looking for a certain magical weapon in this area.
I wonder how they finance themselves. I bet they are secretly trading in some strange stuff.
Make sure to buy some extra weapons before facing that one.
I heard the amazons are after it.
Don't go exploring without weapons. Especially you'll need a machete.
<sigh> The world has grown complicated since my youth.
Selling and buying fine weapons!
I have these offers:
Hello there, adventurer! Need a deal in weapons or armor? I'm your man!
I am the blacksmith. If you need weapons or armor - just ask me.
Great spirit potions as well as health and mana potions in different sizes!
If you need alchemical fluids like slime and blood, get them here.
I sell potions and fluids. If you'd like to see my offers, ask me for a {trade}.
Hm? What is the meaning of all this?
What have I become? What is slime if it's not for everyone?
Slime! Everywhere! SLIME TIME! Or... not?
I AM THE... I mean... I am - what is a mage, if he is not {mad}? If he isn't... raging? I am... I am just sane. A sane mage.
I am not mad... I- YES, that's the whole problem, isn't it? What's going on, what's happening to me? I don't even know anymore.
Yes, well... I'm taking a break. It will take some time. I don't know how long I just... I want to get away from all this for some time, that's it.
Slime is my mission. Is there anything more important? There isn't. To me. Right now at least.
Slime is my mission. Is there anything more important? There isn't. To me. Right now at least.
You want to help me? HELP me? You? Who... who are you anyway? Ah nevermind.
Evil little beasts... I hope someone helps me fight them.
Nasty creepy crawlies!
Hey! You over there, could you help me with a little quest? Just say 'hi' or 'hello' to talk to me!
Don't be shy, can't hurt to greet me with 'hello' or 'hi'!
General equipment and all sorts of goods. Visit my store!
Hey there, adventurer! Need a little rest in my inn? Have some food!
Do you want to sail
We would like to serve you some time.
Do you want to sail
We would like to serve you some time.
Where do you want to go? To {Liberty bay} or to {Nargor}?
I am the captain of this ship.
I am the captain of this ship.
Do you want to sail
We would like to serve you some time.
Where do you want to go? To {Liberty bay} or to {Meriana}?
I am the captain of this ship.
I am the captain of this ship.
There's something about these words which makes me feel awkward. Or maybe it's you who causes that feeling. You better get lost.
Heed. Your. Will. We. Will.
Intruder. Intrude. Must. Explain.
Ssssttttoooopppp.
Our. Master. Is. Gone. You. Can. Not. Visit. Him! We. Stand. {Sentry}!
{Master}. Conducted. Experiments. Great. Problems. You. Must. Go!
{Slime}. Dangerous. We. Have. It. Under. Control. ... We. Will. Stand. {Sentry}.
In this category I have '{Lighthealing}', '{Healfriend}', and '{Curepoison}'.

In this category I have '{Findperson}', '{Magicrope}', '{Levitate}', '{Light}', \z
		'{Greatlight}', '{Haste}', '{Poisonfield}', '{Firefield}', '{Lightmagicmissile}' and '{Energyfield}'.

In this category I have '{Deathstrike}', '{Firewave}', '{Apprenticesstrike}', \z
		'{Energystrike}', '{Terrastrike}', '{Flamestrike}', '{Icestrike}', '{Physicalstrike}', '{Icewave}'.

I can teach you {healing spells}, {support spells} and {attack spells}. \z
		What kind of spell do you wish to learn?
I am Garamond Starstream.

Did you not listen? I am the teacher of druid and sorcerer spells for level 8 to 18. \z
		I teach young adventurers spells they can use once they have the proper vocation - druid or sorcerer.
I have an old friend there. Haven't heard from him in a while.

Oh, it's not too bad here, believe me. At least I always get young and enthusiast disciples! \z
		Though I must confess I miss the vastness of the Tibian plains. <sighs>

A kind old hunter. He loves to see young life around, taking on the ways and lays of the land. \z
		If you have any question, ask Inigo for help.
Roughened and toughened by life's tragedies. A good man, but somber.
Yes, child. If you wish to learn a spell, tell me.

A very headstrong young man, though I appreciate his devotion to the craft of potion-making. \z
		No respect for senior authority or age at all! Except maybe for a little soft spot for Mr Morris.

A strange young man. He seems driven, to my mind. By what force, I do not know. \z
		I take it the world needs adventurers such as him.

Redeeming one's soul by becoming a clerk? Not very likely. Once a pirate, always a pirate. \z
		But he's a charming old rogue.

Not a half bad cook, truly. Must have been that squirrel diet, \z
		it seems to have lead him to discover a new cuisine - everything to forget the bad taste of squirrels, he said.

He is proficient in the martial arts. A very skilled teacher of spells for knights and paladins. \z
		If that is your vocation, you should talk to Ser Tybald.

Ah, yes. Travelled with Plunderpurse a lot as I recall. Captain Plunderpurse, then. \z
			Got his head full of numbers and statistics, that boy.
Newcomers - visit me! I'll answer your questions!
Get some training in the academy!
Feeling lost? Ask me for help!
Gain some knowledge in the academy!
Hello, |PLAYERNAME|. Welcome to the Academy of Rookgaard. May I sign you up as a {student}?
Brilliant! We need fine adventurers like you! If you are ready to learn, just ask me for a lesson. You can always ask for the differently coloured words - such as this one - to continue the lesson.
Brilliant! We need fine adventurers like you! If you are ready to learn, just ask me for a lesson. You can always ask for the differently coloured words - such as this one - to continue the lesson.
Only nonsense on your mind, eh?
Good bye, |PLAYERNAME|! And remember: No running up and down in the academy!
Thank you! Here is your reward.
HEY! You don't have so many!
Go and find some rats to kill!
Have you brought a dead rat to me to pick up your reward?
Thank you! Here is your reward.
HEY! You don't have one! Stop playing tricks on me or I'll give you some extra work!
Go and find some rats to kill!
Do you have a suitable present box for me?
THANK YOU! Here is a helmet that will serve you well.
HEY! You don't have one! Stop playing tricks on me or I'll give you some extra work!
Well, I would like to send our king a little present but I do not have a suitable box. If you find a nice box, please bring it to me.
You are pretty inexperienced. I think killing rats is a suitable challenge for you. For each fresh {dead rat} I will give you two shiny coins of gold.
For this remark I will wash your mouth with soap, young lady!
For this remark I will wash your mouth with soap, young man!
This is an island with {vocation} teachers. You can learn all about the different vocations there once you are level 8.
It's |TIME|, so you are late. Hurry!
My name is Seymour, but for you I am 'Sir' Seymour.
At least you know how to address a man of my importance.
I'm the master of this fine {academy}, giving {lessons} to my students.
Oh, which lesson did you want to attend again? Was it {Rookgaard}, {fighting}, {equipment}, {citizens}, the {academy} or the {oracle}?
In a bank you can deposit your earned gold coins safely. Just go downstairs to {Paulie} and ask him to {deposit} your money.
Yes, depositing your money will keep it safe, so it is a good idea to store it in the bank. Of course, you can always withdraw it again.
Well, the {king} doesn't send troops anymore, the {academy} is dreadfully low on money, and the end of the world is pretty nigh. Apart from that I'm reasonably fine, I suppose.
Most of the citizens here are {merchants}. You can give me the name of any non-player character and I will tell you something about him or her.
Merchants buy and sell goods. Just ask them for a {trade} to see what they offer or buy from you.
Trolls are quite nasty monsters which you shouldn't face before level 3 or 4 depending on your {equipment}. Ask the bridge {guards} for their locations!
The guards {Dallheim} and {Zerbrus} protect our village from {monsters} trying to enter. They also mark useful {dungeon} locations on your map.
There are four vocations: {knights}, {paladins}, {sorcerers} and {druids}. You can choose your vocation once you are level 8 and have talked to the {oracle}.
Sorcerers are talented elemental magicians. You will learn all about them once you are level 8 and reached the Island of {Destiny}.
Knights are strong melee fighters. You will learn all about them once you are level 8 and reached the Island of {Destiny}.
Druids are nature magic users and great healers. You will learn all about them once you are level 8 and reached the Island of {Destiny}.
Paladins are swift distance fighters. You will learn all about them once you are level 8 and reached the Island of {Destiny}.
We have a {weapon} and an {armor} shop south of the academy. {Equipment} such as {ropes} are sold to the north-west. {Potions} can be bought to the south. And then there are the {farms}.
The world of Tibia is very large with tons of places to explore. Vast deserts, Caribbean islands, deep jungles, green meadows and jagged mountains await you!
The temple is the place to go when you are very low on {health} or poisoned. Ask {Cipfried} for a heal - he usually notices emergencies by himself.
Your current health is shown by the red bar on the right side. {Death} awaits you if it goes down to zero.
Dying in Tibia is painful, so try to avoid it. You will lose part of your {experience} points and also equipment. Make sure your {health} always stays up!
You gain experience when fighting {monsters}. You can take a look at your skill window to check your progress.
Good monsters to start hunting are {rats}. They live in the {sewers} below the village.
One entrance to the sewers is south of this {academy}. Look for a sewer grate, then use it to climb down.
The academy is the building you are standing in. We have a {library}, a {bank} and the room of the {oracle}.
There are many books in the bookcases around you, unless some naughty kids stole them again. Read them for more and detailed information.
Don't go hunting without proper equipment. You need at least a suitable {weapon}, {armor}, {shield}, {rope} and {shovel}. A {torch} is also good as well as {legs}, a {helmet} and {shoes}.
Make money by killing {monsters} and picking up their {loot}. You can sell many of the things they carry.
Once a monster is dead, you can select 'Open' on the {corpse} to check what's inside. Sometimes they carry {money} or other items which you can sell to {merchants}.
You can even sell some corpses! For example, you can sell fresh dead rats to {Tom} the tanner or me. He also buys other dead creatures, just ask him for a {trade}.
You definitely need a rope to progress in dungeons, else you might end up stuck. Buy one from {Al Dee} or {Lee'Delle}.
A shovel is needed to dig some {dungeon} entrances open. 'Use' it on a loose stone pile to make a hole large enough to enter.
You should not descend into dungeons without proper {equipment}. Once you are all prepared, ask the bridge {guards} for suitable {monsters}.
A torch will provide you with light in dark {dungeons}. 'Use' it to light it. You can buy them from {Al Dee} or {Lee'Delle}.
Well, I could give you valuable {lessons} or some general {hints} about the game, or a small {quest} if you're interested.
The starter armor, a coat, does not protect you well. First of all, earn some money and try to get a sturdy leather armor from {Dixi}'s  or {Lee'Delle}'s shop. Simply ask for a {trade}.
The starter weapon, a club, won't get you far. You should earn some {money} and buy a better weapon such as a sabre from {Obi}'s or {Lee'Delle}'s shop. Simply ask for a {trade}.
A sturdy leather helmet is a good choice for a beginner. You can either buy it from {Dixi} and {Lee'Delle}, or, once you are strong enough, {loot} them from {trolls}.
I fear you have to buy your first shield by yourself. A wooden shield from {Dixi} or {Lee'Delle} is a good choice.
Leather boots are basic shoes which will protect you well. You can either buy them from {Dixi} and {Lee'Delle}, or, once you are strong enough, {loot} them from {trolls}.
Leather legs might be a good basic protection. You can buy them from {Dixi} or {Lee'Delle}. Or, once you are strong enough, hunt {trolls}. They sometimes carry them in their {loot}.
Many monsters, such as rabbits or deer, are excellent food providers. You can also buy food from {Willie} or {Billy}, the farmers.
Paying for your Tibia account will turn it into a premium account. This means access to more areas and functions of the game as well as other neat features.
Hail to King Tibianus! Long live our king! Not that he cares for an old veteran who is stuck on this godforsaken island...
Use a small health potion in case of emergencies to fill up around 75 health points. You can buy them at {Lily}'s shop. She also has {antidote} potions.
Some monsters poison you. To heal poison, use an antidote potion on yourself. Buy them at {Lily}'s store.
Rookgaard is the name of this {village} as well as of the whole {island}. It belongs to the kingdom of {Thais}, in our world which is called {Tibia}.
The island is separated into a {premium} side and a non-premium side. On both sides you will find {dungeons}, but the premium side tends to be a little less crowded with other players.
The city of Thais is reigned by King Tibianus. Of course, there are many other cities but you will learn about them later.
The most important places in this village are the {temple}, the different {shops}, the {academy} and the {bridges}.
There is a bridge to the north and one to the west which lead outside the village. You should only leave once you are well {equipped} and at least level 2.
You can leave for mainland once you are level 8. To do so talk to the {oracle}.
You have to fight {monsters} to train your {skills} and {level}. If you lose {health}, eat {food} to regain it or use a {potion}.
The more you fight with a weapon, the better will be your skill handling this weapon. Don't worry about that right now though, this will become important once you have a {vocation}.
Once you gained enough experience for a level, you will advance. This means - among other things - more {health} points, a faster walking speed and more strength to carry things.
The farms are west of here. You can buy and sell {food} there which you need to regain {health}.
To attack a rat, simply click on it in your battle list. Make sure that you have proper {equipment}, though! Also, I give you 2 gold coins for each {dead rat}.
I personally don't have anything to trade, but you can ask {merchants} for a trade. That will open a window where you can see their offers and the things they buy from you.
Well, I could give you valuable {lessons} or some general {hints} about the game, or a small {quest} if you're interested.
Shall I try and take a guess at your destiny?
Well, like I told you before, I really think you got that spirit of a sorcerer in you. But of course it's completely up to you!
Well, like I told you before, I really think you got that spirit of a druid in you. But of course it's completely up to you!
Well, like I told you before, I really think you got that spirit of a paladin in you. But of course it's completely up to you!
Well, like I told you before, I really think you got that spirit of a knight in you. But of course it's completely up to you!
Obi sells and buys {weapons}. You can find his shop south of the academy.
Norma has recently opened a bar here meaning she sells drinks and snacks. Nothing of importance to you, young student.
Oh, let's not talk about Loui.
A fine and helpful man. Without him, many new adventurers would be quite clueless.
This is Tom the tanner's mother. Other than that, I don't think she is of importance.
Al Dee has a general {equipment} store in the north-western part of the village. He sells useful stuff such as {ropes}.
A traveller from the {main} continent. I wonder what brought her here. No one comes here of his own free will.
Billy is {Willie}'s cousin, but he has his farm on the {premium} side of the village.
Willie is a fine farmer, although he is short-tempered. He sells and buys {food}.
A humble monk living in the {temple} south of here. He can heal you if you are wounded or poisoned.
Dixi sells and buys {armors}, {shields}, {helmets} and {legs}. You can find her shop south of the academy, just go up the stairs in {Obi}'s shop.
A mysterious druid who lives somewhere in the wilderness. He sells small health {potions} just like {Lily}.
Lee'Delle's shop is in the western part of town, on the {premium} side. She sells everything cheaper.
In the southern part of town, Lily sells {potions} which might come in handy once you are deep in a dungeon and need {health}.
The oracle is a mysterious being just upstairs. It will bring you to the {Island of Destiny} to choose your {vocation} once you are level 8.
Yes, Paulie is very important. He is the local {bank} clerk.
Sir Seymour, yes, that's me.
Tom the tanner buys fresh {corpses}, minotaur leather and paws. Always good to make some {money} if you can carry the corpses there fast enough.
He's the guard on the north {bridge} and a great fighter. He can show you {monster} locations. Just ask him about monsters!
He's the guard on the west {bridge} and a great fighter. He can show you {monster} locations. Just ask him about monsters!
Fine elven armor and weapons. Have a look.
Looking for protective spells for druids? I can teach you.
Greetings, |PLAYERNAME|.
Goodbye, |PLAYERNAME|.
No news are good news.
HAIL TO QUEEN ELOISE!
HAIL TO QUEEN ELOISE!
Just fine.
Would you like to buy the general key to the town?
Yeah, I bet you'd like to do that! HO, HO, HO!
If they make trouble, I'll put them behind bars like all others.
If they make trouble, I'll put them behind bars like all others.
The Bonecrusher family is ideally suited for military jobs.
The Bonecrusher family is ideally suited for military jobs.
If you have a crime to report and clues, then do it, but don't waste my time.
If you have a crime to report and clues, then do it, but don't waste my time.
If you have a crime to report and clues, then do it, but don't waste my time.
If you have a crime to report and clues, then do it, but don't waste my time.
The castle is one of the safest places in Carlin.
Our people are fine and peaceful.
I bet they spy on us... not my business, however.
I scared this bigmouth so much that he left the town by night. HO, HO, HO!
The city is is a peaceful place, and it's up to me to keep it this way.
He is the guy responsible to keep the sewers working. Someone has to do such kind of jobs. I can't handle all the garbage of the city myself.

I found the cause of the initial failure. A rare component, a mirror crystal was used to store magical energy. Miscalculations within the construction damaged this fragile power source. The unleashed energy must have been devastating.

The people of Edron were digging there for minerals and gold. It seemed all that work was of no avail when they finally hit the motherlode. Gems, rare crystals and... water. So much that it flooded the whole system. And not a single soul escaped.

I heard rumours of a fabled creature whose traces were found on an island near Carlin. It lives solely to collect all kinds of rare gems, crystals and minerals. Offering an item it does not already own, reportedly allows passage into its lair.

Our holy ancestor he is. Big as mountain. Lizards say they built palisade to keep him but we not believe ... We think Bong palisade built to have peace from pesky lizards. We respect peace of Bong, keep people away from forbidden land.

Times have changed <sigh>. In the past dragons were feared and respected. Only the {demons} rivalled our notoriety.
Those upstarts! I wonder why would anyone care about them. They lack our style. For them it is all about brute force and showing-off.
Breathing fire is an art! Instead of setting everything on fire, you exhale a cone of fire to give a worthy opponent a chance to avoid it.
Your lives are so short and meaningless and yet you are here! And as a race you even have your own history and remember things with the help of {books}, which amazes me.
I like the idea of books so much that I acquired a human servant to record my memoirs. You find a copy somewhere in my lair.
Lava is only fun as long as it doesn't harden - then it turns into an annoyance.

I am Charkahn the Slayer! The immortal father of the {orcs} and master of this {hive}.
The orcs are the bearers of Blogs rage. This makes us the ultimate fighters and the most powerful of all races.
The orcs are the bearers of Blogs rage. This makes us the ultimate fighters and the most powerful of all races.
I can sense the presence and the feelings of my underlings and {minions}. I embrace the rage of the horde.
The orcish horde of this hive is under my control. I sense their emotions and their needs and provide them with the leadership they need to focus their hate and rage
Hate and rage are the true blessings of Blog, since they are powerful weapons. They give the hive strength. I provide them with direction and focus.
The Raging One blessed us with his burning hate. We are truly his children and therefore {divine}.
To conquer, to destroy and to dominate. Orcs are born to rule the {world}.
One day I will rule the world, even when turned into a {slime}.
Pah! Don't mock me, mortal! This shape is a curse which the evil {djinn} bestowed upon me!
This cursed djinn king! I set him free from an enchanted {lamp}, and he {cheated} me!
Because I freed him he granted me three wishes. He was true to his word in the first two {wishes}.
He built this fortress over Uldrek's grave within a single night. Also, he granted me my second wish and gave me immortality. Test it and try to kill me if you want. Har Har!

I wished to father more healthy and fertile children as any orc has ever done. But the djinn cheated me and made me a slime! Then he laughed at me and left for his abandoned fortress in the {Deathwish} Mountains.

His ancient fortress on Darama was deserted as the evil Djinn fled this world after his imprisonment. Now the time has come for the evil Djinns to return to their master although this will certainly awaken the {good Djinn} too.
I will not share anything more about that topic with you {paleskins}.
You are as ugly as maggots, although not quite as as tasty.
This cursed djinn king! I set him free from an enchanted lamp, and he cheated me!
Uhhhhhh....
It's my curse to be the eternal {guardian} of this ancient {place}.
I'm the {guardian} of the {SEVENTH} and final seal. The seal to open the last door before ... but perhaps it's better to see it with your own eyes.
It served as a temple, a source of power and ... as a sender for an ancient {race} which lived a long time ago and has long been forgotten.
The race that built this edifice came to this place from the stars. They ran from an enemy even more horrible than themselves. But they carried the {seed} of their own destruction in them.
This ancient race was annihilated by its own doings, that's all I know. Aeons have passed since then, but the sheer presence of this {complex} is still defiling and desecrating this area.

Its constructors were too strange for you or even me to understand. We don't know what this ... thing they built was supposed to be good for. I feel a constant twisting and binding of souls, though, that is probably only a side-effect.
The place you know as the Ghostlands had a different name once ... and many names after. Too many to remember them all.
They are my maidens. They give me comfort in my eternal watch over the last seal.

The gods we call the good ones are Fardos, Uman, the Elements, Suon, Crunor, Nornur, Bastesh, Kirok, Toth, and Banor.
Fardos is the creator. The great obsever. He is our caretaker.
Uman is the positive aspect of magic. He brings us the secrets of the arcane arts.
Air is one of the primal elemental forces, sometimes worshipped by tribal shamans.
Fire is one of the primal elemental forces, sometimes worshipped by tribal shamans.
Sula is the essence of the elemental power of water.
Suon is the lifebringing sun. He observes the creation with love.
Crunor, the great tree, is the father of all plantlife. He is a prominent god for many druids.
Nornur is the mysterious god of fate. Who knows if he is its creator or just a chronist?
Bastesh, the deep one, is the goddess of the sea and its creatures.
Kirok, the mad one, is the god of scientists and jesters.
Toth, Lord of Death, is the keeper of the souls, the guardian of the afterlife.
Banor, the heavenly warrior, is the patron of all fighters against evil. He is the gift of the gods to inspire humanity.
The gods we call the evil ones are Zathroth, Fafnar, Brog, Urgith, and the Archdemons!
Zathroth is the destructive aspect of magic. He is the deciver and the thief of souls.
Fafnar is the scorching sun. She observes the creation with hate and jealousy.
Brog, the raging one, is the great destroyer. The berserk of darkness.
The bonemaster Urgith is the lord of the undead and keeper of the damned souls.
The demons are followers of Zathroth. The cruelest are known as the ruthless seven.

You can buy flying carpets only in Darashia.
I transport travellers to the continent of Darama for a small fee. So many want to see the wonders of the desert and learn the secrets of Darama.
I heard too many news to recall them all.
I heard too many news to recall them all.
I can fly you to {Darashia} on Darama, {Kazordoon}, {Svargrond}, {Issavi}, {Farmine} or {Edron} if you like. Where do you want to go?

It's 3:42 pm right now. The next flight is scheduled soon.
Feel the wind in your hair during one of my carpet rides!
Never heard about a place like this.
Hold on!
You shouldn't miss the experience.
I am known as Uzon Ibn Kalith.
I transport travellers to the continent of Darama for a small fee. So many want to see the wonders of the desert and learn the secrets of Darama.
I heard too many news to recall them all.
I heard too many news to recall them all.
It's 3:42 pm right now. The next flight is scheduled soon.
Love is beautiful, we are loved.
I am known as the saleswoman of love, as a cupid.
Im eager for a bath in the lake.
Im interested in shiny precious things, if you have some.
No, you cant have this cloak.
Talk to me if you want to help protecting the village.
Rookgaard needs your help more than ever.
Well, if you change your mind you know where to find me. Remember that if you help Rookgaard, Rookgaard might be able to help you.
OK, dude!
Awesome! Off to work with you. I've marked the barn on your map.
Oh, but you have... you should say {yes}!

No worries, let's refresh your memory. To fight a monster, click on it in the battle list and you'll automatically attack it. It's as easy as that! If you want to practice, just hunt a few harmless rabbits south of here. Remember it now?
Actually I think you have killed enough. You should reply with {yes}!
What do you mean? If you're ready to {help} me again, just say so.
Well, if you change your mind, let me know.
Here, this studded armor will protect you much better. Fits you perfectly! Now - let's work on your footwear. Tom the Tanner can create great boots out of quality leather. You should pay him a visit!

Seriously, don't reject that offer. I'm going to give you a studded armor. No one refuses free stuff! If you don't like it, you can sell it - I don't need it anymore. I promise it's not too used or smelly. Want it?
Then do whatever you have to do first, but please hurry!
Take a small break then and return to me when you have recovered... and cleaned your face.
Alright, then just let me know when you're ready. Don't take too much time though.

This brass helmet will make sure you don't hurt your head. I probably should have given that to you BEFORE you made a rocky tunnel collapse! Take your well-deserved break. Once you're ready for the next mission, talk to me again.

Seriously, don't reject that offer. I'm going to give you a brass helmet. No one refuses free stuff! If you don't like it, you can sell it - I don't need it anymore. I promise there are no fleas in it. Want it?
Let me know when you're ready. This should be fun.
Okay, then good hunting.
Well done, this bone is exactly what I needed! Great. I have to do some preparations, but as reward for your great work, I have a shiny new weapon for you. Here, would you like to have this sword?

Ah... well, if you lost that bone on the way, that's too bad. I just hope you didn't get hungry and nibbled on it. I wouldn't eat cursed flesh if I were you. Anyway, you can still have this old sword as reward, do you want it?
This sword has helped me in many fierce battles. I hope you can put it to good use. Once you're ready for the next mission, talk to me again.
Seriously, don't reject that offer. Just take that sword, it's free. If you don't like it, you can sell it - I don't need it anymore. I promise there are no blood stains on it. Want it?
Well, then just let me know when you're ready.
Alright. Here is the empty flask to use on a dead wasp. I also marked the wasps' nest on your map. Be careful and don't forget to wear your silver amulet for poison protection!
Should I explain it again?
Oh, but there you have it in your inventory! Yeah, your backpack is a bit of a mess. I understand you overlooked it. Dig deeper!
No problem. Here's a new one. I can only give you one per hour though, so try not to lose it again this time.
Great, then please find the wasps' nest, kill one and use the empty flask on its dead body.
Well, please come back with the poison soon. We won't have much time until Kraknaknork's next attack.
Oh, but you don't carry any - did you lose the flask? I can give you a new empty one, but that will also reset your mission, meaning you have to extract new poison. Would you like that?
Alright. Here is the empty flask to use on a dead wasp. Don't lose it this time!
Great, then please find the wasps' nest, kill one and use the empty flask on its dead body.
This brass shield is actually brand-new. It's never been used! I hope it will serve you well. Take a small break, regenerate your health, and then talk to me again for your final mission!
Seriously, don't reject that offer. Just take that shield, it's free. If you don't like it, you can sell it - I don't need it anymore. I promise it's really brand-new. Want it?
Rest for a bit, but don't take too much time to come back.
Alright. Talk to me again to continue with your mission, but heed my words!
Alright.
Have you moved to a new home? I'm the specialist for equipping it.
Don't forget to deposit your money here in the Tibian Bank before you head out for adventure.
Waste not, want not!
Don't burden yourself with too much cash - store it here!
Don't take the money and run - deposit it and walk instead!
We can {change} money for you. You can also access your {bank account}.

There are three different coin types in Tibia: 100 gold coins equal 1 platinum coin, \z
			100 platinum coins equal 1 crystal coin. \z
			So if you'd like to change 100 gold into 1 platinum, simply say '{change gold}' and then '1 platinum'.
We can {change} money for you. You can also access your {bank account}.

Your bank account will be used automatically when you want to {rent} a house or place an offer \z
			on an item on the {market}. Let me know if you want to know about how either one works.

You can check the {balance} of your bank account, {deposit} money or {withdraw} it. \z
			You can also {transfer} money to other characters, provided that they have a vocation.

You can check the {balance} of your bank account, {deposit} money or {withdraw} it. \z
			You can also {transfer} money to other characters, provided that they have a vocation.

You can check the {balance} of your bank account, {deposit} money or {withdraw} it. \z
			You can also {transfer} money to other characters, provided that they have a vocation.
I work in this bank. I can change money for you and help you with your bank account.
I'm afraid this service is not available to you until you reach the World mainland.
Selling weapons, ammunition and armor. Special offers only available here, have a look!
Ah, what the heck.Make sure you know what you want before you bug me.
Buying and selling food!
Make sure you know what you want before you bug me.
You, over there! Stop sniffing around my farm! Either trade with me or leave!
Haven't they taught you anything at school? Ask for a {trade} if you want to trade.
Help yourself. Or ask the other {citizens}, I don't have time for that.
Fine enough.
Willie.
I'm a farmer and a cook.
I try out old and new {recipes}. You can sell all {food} to me.
I'd love to try a banana pie but I lack the {bananas}. If you get me one, I'll reward you.
Which one?
This island would be wonderful if there weren't a constant flood of newcomers.
If I were you, I'd stay here.
I know how to spell.
I'm a magician in the kitchen.
I'm not in the weapon business, so stop disturbing me.
I'm glad that we don't see many officials here.
I'm a farmer, not a preacher.
What about them? Do you live there?
I've got no time for your dungeon nonsense.
My cousin {Billy} cooks rat stew. Yuck! Can you imagine that?
Are you afraid of monsters? I bet even the sight of a {rat} would let your knees tremble. Hahaha.
Do I look like a clock?
I'm a farmer, not a preacher.
Can't stand him.
Quite a babe.
Don't ever mention his name again! He can't even {cook}!
Our little monkey.
Uhm, fine guy I think.
Boring little girl.
Overrated.
She thinks she owns this island with her underpriced offers.
I don't like hippie girls.
Leave me alone with that guy.
About time we got a bar here.
This old guy has only money on his mind.
Hopefully it gets you off this island soon so you can stop bugging me.
Uptight and correct in any situation.
If he wants to sacrifice all his free time for beginners, fine with me. Then they don't disturb me.
This joke of a man thinks he is sooo important.
Decent guy.
Yeah, so?
Overrated.
Too old to be interesting for me.
Have you found a banana for me?
A banana! Great. Here, take this shield, I don't need it anyway.
Are you trying to mess with me?!
Too bad.
No part of clan. Me prefer company of precious. Or mirror image. Always nice to see pretty me!
What you on quest for? Go leave Woblin alone with {precious}

Me not give {key}! Key my precious now! By old goblin law all that one has in his pockets for two days \z
			is family heirloom! Me no part with my precious ... hm unless you provide Woblin with some {reward}!
<mumbles> So where was I again?
<mumbles> Typical - you can never find a hero when you need one!
<mumbles> Could the bonelord language be the invention of some madman?
<mumbles> The curse algorithm of triplex shadowing has to be two times higher than an overcharged nanoquorx on the peripheral...
Trading tokens! First-class equipment available!
The basic bundle for the strike imbuement consists of 20 protective charms. Would you like to buy it for 2 gold tokens??
The intricate bundle for the strike imbuement consists of 20 protective charms and 25 sabreteeth. Would you like to buy it for 4 gold tokens??
The powerful bundle for the strike imbuement consists of 20 protective charms, 25 sabreteeth and 5 vexclaw talons. Would you like to buy it for 6 gold tokens??
The basic bundle for the vampirism imbuement consists of 25 vampire teeth. Would you like to buy it for 2 gold tokens??
The intricate bundle for the strike imbuement consists of 20 protective charms and 25 sabreteeth. Would you like to buy it for 4 gold tokens??
The powerful bundle for the vampirism imbuement consists of 25 vampire teeth, 15 bloody pincers and 5 pieces of dead brain. Would you like to it for 6 gold tokens??
The basic bundle for the void imbuement consists of 25 rope belts. Would you like to buy it for 2 gold tokens??
The intricate bundle for the void imbuement consists of 25 rope belts and 25 silencer claws. Would you like to buy it for 4 gold tokens??.
The powerful bundle for the void imbuement consists of 25 rope belts, 25 silencer claws and 5 grimeleech wings. Would you like to buy it for 6 gold tokens??

Fine. You are free to decline my offer.
Keep your adventurer's stone well.
Ah, you want to replace your adventurer's stone for free?
Here you are. Take care.
No problem.
Ah, you want to replace your adventurer's stone for 30 gold?
Here you are. Take care.
Sorry, you don't have enough money.
No problem.
I think you have forgotten to bring your stake.
Yes, I was informed what to do. Are you prepared to receive my line of the prayer?
So receive my prayer: 'Protection will be granted - from dangers at hand'. Now, bring your stake to Isimov in the dwarven settlement for the next line of the prayer. I will inform him what to do.
I will wait for you.
You should visit Isimov in the dwarven settlement now.
You already received my line of the prayer.
A blessed stake? That is a strange request. Maybe Quentin knows more, he is one of the oldest monks after all.
You are hurt, my child. I will heal your wounds.
You aren't looking that bad. Sorry, I can't help you. But if you are looking for additional protection you should go on the {pilgrimage} of ashes or get the protection of the {twist of fate} here.

Whenever you receive a lethal wound, your vital force is damaged and there is a chance that you lose some of your equipment. With every single of the five {blessings} you have, this damage and chance of loss will be reduced.

There are five blessings available in five sacred places: the {spiritual} shielding, the spark of the {phoenix}, the {embrace} of Tibia, the fire of the {suns} and the wisdom of {solitude}. Additionally, you can receive the {twist of fate} here.
I see you received the spiritual shielding in the whiteflower temple south of Thais.
I can sense that the druids north of Carlin have provided you with the Embrace of Tibia.
I can see you received the blessing of the two suns in the suntower near Ab'Dendriel.
I can sense that the spark of the phoenix already was given to you by the dwarven priests of earth and fire in Kazordoon.
I can sense you already talked to the hermit Eremo on the isle of Cormaya and received this blessing.
You can ask for the blessing of spiritual shielding in the whiteflower temple south of Thais.
The druids north of Carlin will provide you with the embrace of Tibia.
You can ask for the blessing of the two suns in the suntower near Ab'Dendriel.
The spark of the phoenix is given by the dwarven priests of earth and fire in Kazordoon.
Talk to the hermit Eremo on the isle of Cormaya about this blessing.
Beds, chairs, tables, statues and everything you could imagine for decorating your home!
I have {beds}, {chairs}, {containers}, {decoration}, {flowers}, {instruments}, {pillows}, {pottery}, {statues}, {tapestries} and {tables}. Which of those would you like to see?

In this category I have '{Bruise Bane}', '{Cure Bleeding}', '{Wound Cleansing}', \z
		'{Cure Poison}', '{Intense Wound Cleansing}', '{Recovery}' and '{Intense Recovery}'.

In this category I have '{Light}', '{Find Person}', '{Magic Rope}', '{Levitate}', '{Haste}', \z
		'{Charge}' and '{Great Light}'.

I can teach you {healing spells}, {attack spells} and {support spells}. \z
		What kind of spell do you wish to learn? \z
			You can also tell me for which level you would like to learn a spell, if you prefer that.
<sigh> lost... word...
<sigh> ohhhh.... memories...
The secrets... too many... sleep...
Loneliness...

Are you injured or poisoned? I can help you.
For Rookgaard! For Tibia!
No monster shall go past me.
The premium side of Rookgaard lies beyond.
Want to know what monsters are good for you at your level? Just ask me!
Greetings, |PLAYERNAME|! You're looking really bad. Let me heal your wounds.
<nods> At your service, |PLAYERNAME|, protecting the {village} from {monsters}.
Bye, |PLAYERNAME|.

Hmm, |PLAYERNAME|. I really don't know if you should go into the wilderness without a shield. You should earn some money by hunting rats under the village first until you can afford at least a wooden shield.
Zerbrus, at your service.
I'm the guard of this bridge. I defend Rookgaard against the beasts of the {wilderness} and the {dungeons}, and even of the stinking {sewers}.
My duty is eternal. Time is of no importance.
Before you fight monsters, deposit your money there. If you die, you won't lose it if it's save in the bank.
I have to stay here, sorry. But I can {heal} you if you are wounded. I have also valuable information about {monsters}.
I'm an expert concerning the wildlife and {monsters} on this island.
I'm not a gossiper, but if you want to know something about a certain Rookgaardian, ask.
They're not that dangerous, but don't let yourself get cornered by many of them. They still can hurt.
There are two types of spiders on this island: normal spiders and poison spiders. As their name says, the latter ones can poison you which will cause a constant loss of your health. Beware of them!
Once you have upgraded your starter {equipment} a little - maybe leather stuff - and got a better weapon as well as a shield, you should be able to kill trolls.
You need a stronger weapon than the starter club to kill wolves. Maybe a {sabre} or a short {sword} will do.
The orcs have a hideout under the troll cave, just descend deep. Still, be very careful!
Minotaurs are very strong. You will need a group of people or at least level 6 and good equipment to be able to kill them.
They are a little tougher than they look like, but they're still good beginner monsters.
The skeletons on this island are usually fairly hidden. They are often found near graves and always underground. Beware of their evil life drain.
There is a bear cave close to this bridge. Be careful, though, they hit hard!
Wasp hives exist only behind the north bridge, so ask {Dallheim} for information. I'm glad there are no wasps here. They always make me jump.
Snakes can poison you, too, but their poison is usually not as dangerous as that of poison spiders. Just be careful and kill them fast.
Premium warriors pay. They gain many advantages this way.
HAIL TO THE KING!
It's north of the temple.
A place of protection. Nothing can harm you there.
I can mark you some dungeons on your map if you ask me about {monsters} suited for your level.
The sewers are crowded with {rats}. They make fine targets for young heroes.
There are {wolves}, {bears}, {snakes}, {deer}, and {spiders} in the wilderness past this bridge.
I'm a follower of {Banor}.
The heavenly warrior! Read {books} to learn about him.
You will learn about magic soon enough.
In the world of Tibia, many challenges await the brave adventurer.
There are many books in the {academy}. Theory can be just as important as practice.
It's my duty to protect this village and its {citizens}.
It looks like you have mastered the drill.
Sorry, I don't trade. Ask the shop owners for a trade instead.
I don't approve of her opening a bar. Those drunken adventurers stumbling over the bridge at nighttime are not especially helpful in improving the situation.
Such a chicken-hearted person.
He sells {weapons}. His shop is south west of town, close to the {temple}.
He dedicated his life to welcoming newcomers to this island.
She has her own problems.
There's something about this guy I don't like.
She's very attractive. Too bad my duty leaves me no time to date her.
He guards the temple and can heal you if you are badly injured or poisoned, just like me.
She's {Obi's} granddaughter and deals with {armors} and {shields}. Her shop is south west of town, close to the temple.
One of these reclusive druids.
Her shop is just north of the bridge. She really has good offers.
Gentle girl, but a little to esoteric for my taste. However, her {potions} might come in handy.
You can find the oracle on the top floor of the {academy}, just above {Seymour}. Go there when you are level 8.
The local {bank} clerk.
His job of teaching young heroes is important for the survival of all of us.
He's the local tanner. You could try selling fresh corpses or leather to him.
He does a fine job, protecting the north bridge. He's been on duty longer than me and knows the north side of Rookgaard well.
At your service.
He's a farmer. A little more friendly than his cousin {Willie}, he also buys and sells {food}.
He's quite rude. Also, he buys and sells {food}.
You are poisoned. I will help you.
You are looking really bad. Let me heal your wounds.
You aren't looking really bad. Eat some food to regain strength.
Come over here if you have to resupply!
I wish someone could spare a minute and help me...
This is too hard for an old woman like me.
Hello, young adventurer, you look strong enough to help me!
Feel the wind in your hair during one of my carpet rides!
Never heard about a place like this.

We would like to serve you some time.

Don't forget to deposit your money here in the Global Bank before you head out for adventure.
I sell equipment for your adventure! Just ask me for a {trade} to see my wares.
I wait. I wait for someone like you to come here. I wait for them to grow disconsolate. I wait for them to despair. And I wait for them to die. Muhahaha.
I am Zumtah, Zumtah the impeccable, Zumtah the marvellous, Zumtah the... the... eternal.
The land you are currently dwelling in, human. Don't you have any sense of your surroundings?
I have seen many of them. I have seen many of them die. In here, with me. Perhaps you will be pleased to meet them. Not long and you will join their ranks. Muhaha.
Pesky creatures. Many of them have been brought here, many of them died here. Humans, lizards, beasts, they all die the same. Down here, with me. Muhahaha.
What? What do you mean by that?
Hmm, an old one. I don't care much about politics or power, as here, he has none. Here, only I have power. Muhaha.
What are you talking about, such things do not matter down here. Down here alone, isolated and broken. Muhaha.
